title: Sweet Smoky Tunisian Fig Tagine
description: A pungent and fluffy Tunisian dish featuring figs and robata-grilled ingredients, perfect for a sick day, and macrobiotic-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced onion to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Grill the onion mixture on the robata grill for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a large clay or ceramic tagine, combine the grilled onion mixture, soaked figs, and chicken broth.
Cover the tagine and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Remove the tagine from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
